description: The service exits immediately on SIGTERM, dropping in-flight requests or unacked jobs on every deploy.
body: Failure mode: SIGTERM kills the process while handlers are mid-flight, so in-flight HTTP requests fail and unacked jobs are lost or, if already acked, never completed. Detection signals: no signal handler or one that calls exit immediately; readiness does not flip before exit; users see errors during every rollout. Do not depend on the orchestrator's hard kill. Safer fallback: trap SIGTERM, stop accepting work, drain in-flight handlers within a bounded deadline, re-queue the rest, and add a SIGTERM-under-load test.
